AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS AT 100 MPH...A PATH LENGTH OF 1.8 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF 400 YARDS...TOUCHED DOWN IN THE RAMBLEWOOD SUBDIVISION JUST SOUTH OF ROGERSVILLE ALABAMA. SEVERAL HOMES WERE SLIGHTLY DAMAGED...NUMEROUS TREES WERE EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AND TWO UTILITY POLES WERE SNAPPED IN THE PATH. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR ROGERSVILLE ALONG FUQUA LANE.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE REPORTED AT LUCYS BRANCH RESORT AND MARINA. BOATS WERE OVERTURNED, POWERLINES WERE DOWN, AND ROOFTOPS WERE BLOWN OFF AT THIS LOCATION.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 105 MPH...A PATH WIDTH OF 450 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF 10.54 MILES TOUCHED DOWN ALONG COUNTY ROAD 388 IN NORTHERN LAWRENCE COUNTY. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST ALONG THE ELK RIVER AND PRODUCED MINOR DAMAGE TO A MARINA...SEVERAL HOMES AND NUMEROUS TREES. THE TORNADO LIFTED IN SOUTHERN LIMESTONE COUNTY ALONG CURTIS ROAD.

*** 1 INJ *** ANOTHER BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE ANDERSON COMMUNITY OF EASTERN LAUDERDALE COUNTY. IT FIRST AFFECTED AREAS ALONG HWY 207 SOUTH OF CR 86 WHERE MULTIPLE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. ONE POWER POLE WAS ALSO SNAPPED AT THIS LOCATION NEAR ITS BASE. JUST NORTHEAST, SEVERAL WELL-BUILT HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE. ONE MINOR INJURY OCCURRED HERE AS THE TORNADO REACHED ITS MAX INTENSITY OF 105 MPH. ADDITIONAL TREES WERE SNAPPED JUST BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED EAST OF CR 530.
POWER LINES DOWN AND COMPLETELY BLOCKING THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 562 AND COUNTY ROAD 70.

A SHORT-LIVED EF-1 TORNADO AFFECTED THE SMALL COMMUNITY OF CARTWRIGHT IN LIMESTONE COUNTY. HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG HARRIS LOOP ROAD. POWER POLES WERE ALSO SNAPPED. ESTIMATED WINDS WERE DETERMINED TO BE AROUND 100 MPH. ONE HOME SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE TO ITS ROOF AS IT WAS PARTIALLY LIFTED AND SHINGLES WERE MISSING. THE TORNADO LIFTED ALONG THE HILLTOPS JUST WEST OF WITTY MILLS RD.

10 TREES DOWN ACROSS PROPERTY/FARM. SOME UPROOTED BUT ALL APPEAR TO BE FALLING ONE DIRECTION. DELAYED REPORT RELAYED TO NWS BY LINCOLN COUNTY EMA.
DAMAGE ON JESSY LANE ROAD NEAR HARVEST ROAD IN HARVEST. 2 LARGE TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN. NUMEROUS SHINGLES TAKEN OFF ROOFS. PLAYGROUND EQUIPMENT TOSSED.

DAMAGE REPORTED NEAR THE 3500 BLOCK OF GRIFFIN ROAD. THE ROOF OF A BACK PORCH WAS TORN OFF AND BLOWN SEVERAL HUNDRED YARDS. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED.

THIS TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL BARN AND FARM STRUCTURES ALONG MARBLE HILL ROAD TEARING THE SIDE COMPLETELY OFF OF ONE. A WELL BUILT HOME SUSTAINED VERY MINOR SIDING DAMAGE. LARGE HARDWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED ALONG ITS 1.6 MILE TRACK, A FEW SNAPPED AND TWISTED NEAR THE BASE. THE TORNADO APPARENTLY LIFTED AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS HWY 50 AND INTO THE HIGHER TERRAIN OF BEAN HOLLOW. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH PEAK WINDS OF 95 MPH.
DAMAGE ON MARBLE HILL ROAD. SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED.

DAMAGE ALONG OLD HOLLOW ROAD AND ABOUT 1/4-1/2 MILE ENE. ROOF ONE ONE SHED WAS BLOWN INTO A HOME PRODUCING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. ANOTHER TIN ROOF OF A SHED WAS BLOWN ONTO A HOME. AN APPROX 20 BY 80 HAY BARN WAS BLOWN ABOUT 100 YARDS ONTO A HOME PRODUCING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE.

DAMAGE ALONG OLD HOLLOW ROAD AND ABOUT 1/4-1/2 MILE ENE. ROOF ONE ONE SHED WAS BLOWN INTO A HOME PRODUCING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. ANOTHER TIN ROOF OF A SHED WAS BLOWN ONTO A HOME. AN APPROX 20 BY 80 HAY BARN WAS BLOWN ABOUT 100 YARDS ONTO A HOME PRODUCING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE.

THIS VERY BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AROUND THE TIMS FORD LAKE AREA JUST WEST OF WILEY ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. A LARGE OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED AND A HOME SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. ANOTHER HOME SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WHEN A ROOF OFF A COMPLETELY DESTROYED OUTBUILDING WENT THROUGH THE SIDE OF THE HOME. NO ONE WAS INJURED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST BEFORE LIFTING WEST OF HADDON LANE. BASED ON THE DAMAGE, THIS TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH PEAK WINDS OF 95 MPH.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE ESTILL SPRINGS/WILDER CHAPEL COMMUNITIES IN NORTHEAST FRANKLIN COUNTY TN. THIS SHORT-LIVED TORNADO INITIALLY SNAPPED TREES ALONG AND WEST OF OAK GROVE RD. ALONG WILDER CHAPEL ROAD...A LARGE SWATH OF HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TWISTED NEAR THE BASE. A LARGE OUTBUILDING WAS ALSO DESTROYED AND A HOUSE SUFFERED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. THIS IS WHERE THE TORNADO REACHED MAXIMUM INTENSITY OF AN EF-1 WITH PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH.
SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ON WILDERS CHAPEL ROAD.

TRUCK TRAILER REPORTED BLOWN ON TOP OF AN INDUSTRIAL BUILDING IN NORTH FORT PAYNE AT THE INTERSECTION OF CR 137 AND EXIT 224 ON I-59. DAMAGE ALSO REPORTED AT AN APARTMENT COMPLEX NORTHEAST OF THIS LOCATION. ROOF BLOWN OFF AND 25-30 PEOPLE DISPLACED. NO INJURIES REPORTED.

*** 1 INJ *** A BRIEF BUT STRONG TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTHEAST OF FORT PAYNE FLIPPING SEVERAL LARGE 18-WHEEL TRAILERS AND RESULTING IN SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A RECYCLING PLANT AND ADJACENT METAL BUILDING SYSTEM. JUST DOWNSTREAM...THE TORNADO DID SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO TWO RESIDENCES WHERE UPLIFT OF ROOF AND COLLAPSE OF EXTERIOR WALLS WERE NOTED. THIS IS WHERE THE TORNADO WAS AT ITS PEAK INTENSITY OF 125MPH. ALONG ITS 0.8 MILE TRACK, NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SOME SNAPPED NEAR ITS BASE.

DAMAGE WAS FIRST NOTED JUST SOUTH OF BIRD DOG TRAIL WHERE A 40X50FT BARN COLLAPSED AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. TIN FROM THE ROOF OF THE BARN WAS SEEN TOSSED UP TO 1/3 MILE AWAY. THE SHORT-LIVED TORNADO WAS AT ITS PEAK INTENSITY /100 MPH/ AT THIS LOCATION. ADDITIONAL TREES BETWEEN THIS LOCATION AND THE INTERSECTION OF PUMPKIN CENTER ROAD AND CR 153/FLETCHER ROAD. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST PAST THIS POINT AS NO ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS SEEN IN RURAL/HIGHER TERRAIN.

FLOODING OCCURRED ALLONG THE ALLEN BRANCH BASIN IN VALLEY HEAD. RAINFALL OF 4.69 INCHES OCCURRED AT THE NWS HCN/COOP SITE. RAILROAD TRACKS WERE WASHED AWAY STRANDING A TRAIN. UP TO 2.5 FEET OF WATER FLOODED THE FIRE DEPARTMENT AND UP TO 1 FOOT OF WATER FLOODED CITY HALL. TIME ESTIMED.

A BROKEN LINE OF STRONG/SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT WIND DAMAGE IN THE CITY OF RED BAY. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AT THE BASE FROM RED BAY HIGH SCHOOL NORTHEASTWARD. A 400-YD WIDE SWATH OF DAMAGE CONTINUED FOR 2.7 MI...WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH.
